entries.compatibilityNotes: The native component keeps the standalone entry point but uses direct controlled props instead of an options object.
The provider remains compositional but owns only cross-board gesture, layout, and overlay coordination.
The inferred 46-property browser context is intentionally private and has no public native hook.
entries.rationale: The upstream hook leaks internal shadow position, annotation, and browser-drag state; exporting it would make those implementation details a compatibility contract.

entries.compatibilityNotes: The native spare piece is provider-coordinated and identifies both its spare source and target board.
CSS grid defaults become React Native theme/style defaults.
Flexbox-compatible square defaults move into the native theme.
The default dark-square color is preserved in the native theme.
The default light-square color is preserved in the native theme.
The CSS inset shadow becomes a native drop-target state style.
Notation contrast remains theme-driven with React Native text styles.
Notation contrast remains theme-driven with React Native text styles.
Absolute CSS text placement becomes native notation layout.
Absolute CSS text placement becomes native notation layout.
The lift transform becomes a Reanimated drag-overlay style.
The source ghost opacity remains configurable in the native theme.
All ten geometry and opacity defaults are retained in native annotation styling.
The twelve standard piece keys remain, but published defaults use original permissive artwork rather than the fixture-only Cburnett set.
The grid generator uses validated dimensions and canonical square identifiers.
The orientation-aware conversion remains a pure coordinate utility.
The conversion remains pure and is bounded to files a through z.
The inverse conversion remains pure and validates canonical file identifiers.
The inverse conversion remains pure and validates ranks 1 through 99.
The native parser is strict, atomic, and permits FEN only for an 8x8 board.
entries.rationale: The upstream parser silently accepts malformed rows, treats multi-digit empties one digit at a time, and performs no dimension validation; native invalid input must fail atomically.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Unique upstream movement heuristics remain only as a visual fallback; ambiguous ties fade or snap.
entries.rationale: The upstream utility embeds partial chess heuristics and chooses the first object-iteration candidate on ties, which can animate the wrong identical piece.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Promotion inference is a safe fallback behind explicit transition hints and piece IDs.
entries.rationale: Promotion and reversal inference is presentation-only and cannot decide game semantics or manufacture a canonical move.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Coordinates derive from measured rectangular native board geometry, not DOM width.
DOM element lookup and React.TouchEvent hit testing have no public native equivalent.
entries.rationale: Native input uses one board-level gesture surface and measured coordinate hit testing; exposing a document-based helper would be unusable and misleading.

entries.compatibilityNotes: The exact options object belongs only to the future react-chessboard compatibility subpath; the primary API uses direct props.
Compatibility arrows map to ID-bearing controlled native annotations.
Square identity and light/dark parity remain pure derived geometry.
Piece data keeps an open pieceType and adds optional consumer-owned identity.
Move payloads distinguish board and spare sources and carry revision/intent correlation.
entries.rationale: A durable dragging-piece value lacks board identity, controlled base revision, input modality, and cancellation correlation required by the native intent lifecycle.

entries.compatibilityNotes: The type becomes a deeply readonly sparse record; canonical square validation lands with Phase 1 normalization.
Native square activation adds board, revision, selection, action, and input context.
Native payloads use typed board/spare sources and current controlled revision context.
The move intent carries identity, source kind, target, revision, and input modality.
entries.rationale: The upstream payload and synchronous Boolean callback cannot represent async validation, controlled commit correlation, cancellation, or spare-to-board routing.

entries.compatibilityNotes: The native renderer is visual-only and receives size plus resolved interaction state without owning handlers.
Native renderers receive piece, square, size, and interaction state instead of SVG CSS props.
The twelve standard FEN piece-placement codes remain unchanged.
Renamed to required, non-empty, provider-unique boardId.
Whole-object renderer replacement remains explicit; published defaults use permissive native artwork.
The prop is the only canonical position and may be plain or revisioned.
entries.rationale: Upstream stores a mutable currentPosition mirror and mutates presentation snapshots; native rendering must always derive semantics from the latest controlled prop.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Renamed to orientation; canonical square IDs never change.
Rows are validated integers from 1 through 99.
Columns are validated integers from 1 through 26.
React.CSSProperties becomes a React Native StyleProp with documented precedence.
Base square styling becomes a React Native StyleProp.
Per-square styles remain declarative and keyed by canonical square.
Dark-square overrides move into the native theme/style contract.
Light-square overrides move into the native theme/style contract.
The drop-target state is exposed to native styles/renderers.
The active overlay style is animated on the UI thread.
The source ghost style remains configurable.
CSS text properties become native TextStyle values.
CSS text properties become native TextStyle values.
File-label styling uses native layout and typography.
Rank-label styling uses native layout and typography.
The Boolean notation toggle is retained.
Renamed and applied only to presentation transitions.
Animation policy honors system reduced motion and latest-wins transitions.
entries.rationale: A Boolean browser animation switch cannot express the required system, always, and never reduced-motion policy or deterministic interruption semantics.

entries.compatibilityNotes: The primary board is move-read-only without onMoveRequest; permissions gate eligible inputs.
This remains a visual drag-overlay bounds policy; it does not decide whether a null-target move intent is accepted.
No programmatic ancestor scrolling is promised for 1.0; gesture arbitration is still required.
entries.rationale: Portable React Native code cannot generically control an arbitrary ancestor ScrollView while preserving deterministic destination geometry.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Mapped to the native gesture activation threshold.
Drawing is enabled by a controlled native tool and operation handler.
entries.rationale: The upstream gate controls an internal arrow list; native drawing must emit revisioned operations without accepting semantic state inside the component.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Arrows become ID-bearing controlled annotations, with at most one transient draft.
entries.rationale: Combining external arrows with a persistent internal list creates dual ownership and cannot safely reconcile concurrent store updates.

entries.compatibilityNotes: All ten geometry/style controls map to native SVG annotation options; the object remains whole-value replacement.
A policy emits a revisioned clear operation naming only observed annotation IDs.
entries.rationale: The board cannot clear consumer-owned annotations itself; emitting a scoped operation avoids overwriting annotations added concurrently.

entries.compatibilityNotes: The policy is independent of click clearing and emits a reasoned controlled operation.
entries.rationale: Upstream accidentally gates position-change clearing on clearArrowsOnClick and mutates internal state; native policy must be independent and store-driven.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Retained as a synchronous permission callback with typed board/spare source context.
The compatibility adapter reduces one operation against latest controlled arrows; the primary API emits the operation.
entries.rationale: Emitting whole internal-arrow snapshots on effects can overwrite concurrent controlled updates and fires even for the initial empty state.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Pointer hover compatibility is deferred to post-1.0 React Native Web work.
entries.rationale: Touch-first Android and iOS have no mouse-out event, and browser hover ordering is not a portable native contract.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Pointer hover compatibility is deferred to post-1.0 React Native Web work.
entries.rationale: Touch-first Android and iOS have no mouse-over event, and browser hover ordering is not a portable native contract.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Mapped onto one authoritative press pipeline; it never bubbles into a duplicate semantic callback.
Mapped to native drag start with current controlled revision context.
The primary callback is async/cancellable; compatibility maps Boolean true/false exactly once.
entries.rationale: A synchronous Boolean result cannot correlate acceptance with a later controlled commit, cancel stale promises, or handle timeouts deterministically.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Mapped to an orientation-aware touch/keyboard/accessibility activation intent.
Mapped where supported to portable press-in data without React.MouseEvent.
Mapped where supported to portable press-out data without React.MouseEvent.
Right-click compatibility is post-1.0; native annotation uses long-press, two-finger, or explicit mode.
entries.rationale: Secondary-button semantics and browser event ordering do not exist consistently on the first-class Android and iOS targets.

entries.compatibilityNotes: The visual-only native renderer receives piece, square, size, styles, and interaction state but cannot own gestures or accessibility.
A standalone board creates a private native provider; an existing provider is reused.
The native provider owns only gesture coordination, layout registration, and a shared overlay.
entries.rationale: The upstream context exposes canonical-looking position and arrow state plus browser implementation details, which would recreate dual ownership.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Spare pieces require a provider and identify spareId, targetBoardId, and piece.
Required stable boardId drives a provider registry; no DOM IDs are generated.
Validated dimensions use canonical square IDs; orientation changes visual order only.
FEN is strict and 8x8-only; variants use PositionObject and non-placement fields are intentionally discarded.
entries.rationale: The upstream parser accepts malformed and dimension-mismatched input, so retaining it would violate atomic validation and predictable variant behavior.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Rendering always uses the latest normalized controlled prop and never a durable mirror.
entries.rationale: A mutable shadow position can diverge from the consumer store during gestures and animations, creating the second source of truth this port exists to remove.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Changes cancel interaction and transition epochs before reconciling the latest controlled input.
entries.rationale: Directly replacing a local position without cancelling asynchronous work allows stale gestures and timers to overwrite newer geometry.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Transitions are deterministic presentation and honor the native reduced-motion policy.

upstream.name: External diff and one-deep timeout queue

entries.compatibilityNotes: Revisioned latest-wins transitions replace the one-deep timeout queue.
entries.rationale: The upstream queue can commit stale snapshots after rapid updates because timeouts and effect dependencies do not correlate every update to an epoch.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Pending intent presentation never changes semantics; only a new controlled position commits.
entries.rationale: Suppressing animation through internal manual-drop flags treats a gesture result as accepted state before the consumer has committed it.

entries.compatibilityNotes: An explicit rookMove hint animates castling without fabricating a logical position.
entries.rationale: A fake intermediate position temporarily renders state the consumer never supplied and can become stale when another update interrupts the timeout.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Explicit hints and piece IDs take precedence; only a safe unambiguous promotion fallback animates.
A tie is explicitly ambiguous and fades/snaps; unique upstream piece heuristics remain fallback hints.
entries.rationale: Object iteration order is not evidence of piece identity, so selecting the first identical candidate can animate a legal-looking but incorrect move.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Pure geometry and Reanimated support files a-z and ranks 1-99 without DOM transforms.
Every update, unmount, orientation, dimension, and permission change invalidates timers and epochs.
entries.rationale: The pinned effect omits state and option dependencies and only clears the latest timeout on cleanup, allowing stale closures to settle obsolete state.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Direct permissions remain; without onMoveRequest the primary board is move-read-only.
RNGH handles touch/tap/drag; keyboard/accessibility input and second-finger cancel are first-class.
1.0 preserves ScrollView arbitration but never programmatically scrolls an ancestor.
entries.rationale: React Native cannot generically discover and control arbitrary ancestor scrolling while retaining a stable measured destination.

entries.compatibilityNotes: One board-level coordinate hit test and epoch-correlated remeasurement replace DOM collision detection.
A single interaction epoch drives the shared native drag overlay.
The async intent lifecycle owns decision, cancellation, timeout, and controlled commit; compatibility maps Boolean results once.
entries.rationale: The upstream callback cannot represent pending validation or correlate a true result with the later authoritative position update.

entries.compatibilityNotes: targetSquare null follows the same decision and commit lifecycle as an on-board target.
entries.rationale: Ignoring the off-board Boolean return creates different acceptance semantics for the same callback and can falsely present a removal as accepted.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Second-finger and accessibility cancel abort the active interaction epoch.
Native overlay policy and final remeasurement replace DOM transform clamping.
Native overlay, source ghost, and pending styles preserve the useful visual states.
One authoritative pipeline prevents one press from emitting duplicate semantic intents.
entries.rationale: Browser bubbling makes piece clicks also reach the square handler and contradicts documented conditions; native intent ordering must be explicit.

entries.compatibilityNotes: A board-level tap recognizer uses measured coordinates instead of document lookup.
Hover is post-1.0 RN Web work; native pressed/drag-over state is renderer data.
entries.rationale: Android and iOS touch input has no portable hover lifecycle, and exact synthesized browser event ordering is outside the native support tier.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Portable press-in/out callbacks carry native data rather than React.MouseEvent.
Long-press and native annotation gestures replace right-click for 1.0.
entries.rationale: Secondary-button context menus, preventDefault, and DOM bubbling order are browser-specific and deferred to the post-1.0 web tier.

entries.compatibilityNotes: A typed spare source carries spareId and resolves the target board revision at emission.
entries.rationale: Using piece type as a pseudo-square is ambiguous, conflicts with the declared nullable square payload, and cannot route multiple boards.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Callback payloads come from the current normalized controlled snapshot and include its revision.
entries.rationale: Reading callback pieces from a mutable mirror can report data from a stale animation snapshot instead of the consumer's authoritative position.

upstream.name: External internal and draft arrow composition

entries.compatibilityNotes: Rendered annotations equal the controlled collection plus at most one ephemeral draft.
entries.rationale: A second persistent internal arrow list makes rendering and callbacks diverge from the controlled collection and cannot safely merge concurrent changes.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Long-press, two-finger, and explicit-mode gestures emit one controlled annotation operation.
1.0 uses an explicit portable palette; Shift-over-Control behavior is compat/web work.
entries.rationale: Hardware keyboard modifier and secondary-button combinations are not consistently available on first-class Android and iOS touch targets.

upstream.name: Internal arrow toggle and external duplicate refusal

entries.compatibilityNotes: ID-based add/toggle/remove operations include base revision and deterministic geometry matching.
entries.rationale: Index-based internal toggling cannot represent duplicate controlled arrows or safely modify a store collection that changed during the gesture.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Primary code emits an operation; compatibility reduces it against latest arrows and requires controlled feedback.
entries.rationale: Effect-driven snapshots fire for the initial empty list and can overwrite controlled annotations that arrived after a gesture began.

entries.compatibilityNotes: A clear operation names IDs observed at its base revision and includes reason board-press.
entries.rationale: Clearing internal state cannot clear controlled arrows and offers no safe concurrency semantics for a store-owned annotation collection.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Position clearing is an independent policy operation and is never gated by click-clearing configuration.
entries.rationale: The upstream clearArrows helper checks clearArrowsOnClick, so clearArrowsOnPositionChange silently fails when click clearing is disabled.

upstream.name: Arrow coordinates orientation and rectangular boards

entries.compatibilityNotes: Pure 2048-wide geometry scales height by rows/columns and handles both orientations.

upstream.name: Straight and knight arrow path selection

entries.compatibilityNotes: Both shapes remain, but knight detection uses integer file/rank deltas rather than exact floating hypot.

upstream.name: Target shortening and active draft exemption

entries.compatibilityNotes: Endpoint and same-target shortening remain, and the active draft stays exempt.

upstream.name: Arrow width opacity offset layers and marker IDs

entries.compatibilityNotes: RN SVG draws deterministic paths/heads in below/above layers without global marker IDs.

upstream.name: Board square and drop style precedence

entries.compatibilityNotes: A documented React Native StyleProp order replaces CSS object spread precedence.
Native Text renders oriented file/rank edges with explicit theme and override precedence.
The visual-only renderer receives resolved style, size, piece, and interaction state under explicit layer precedence.
entries.rationale: Upstream custom square rendering bypasses squareStyles and can own DOM children/handlers, making styling and interaction behavior dependent on fallback selection.

entries.compatibilityNotes: Custom renderers receive square, native size/state, and the published default set uses original permissive artwork; Cburnett stays fixture-only.

upstream.name: Rules promotion decisions and premove absence

entries.compatibilityNotes: The board remains rules-free: consumers own legality, promotion decisions, premoves, selection, history, and game state.

upstream.name: DOM CSS dnd-kit and accessibility absence

entries.compatibilityNotes: View, RNGH, Reanimated, and RN SVG replace browser primitives; one adjustable board control and reduced motion are first-class.
entries.rationale: Copying DOM, CSS, dnd-kit sensors, and absent accessibility semantics would not produce a native component or satisfy TalkBack and VoiceOver contracts.
